title: Validate Binary Tree Nodes
content:
You have n binary tree nodes numbered from 0 to n - 1 where node i has two children leftChild[i] and rightChild[i], return true if and only if all the given nodes form exactly one valid binary tree.

If node i has no left child then leftChild[i] will equal -1, similarly for the right child.

Note that the nodes have no values and that we only use the node numbers in this problem.

Example 1:

Input: n = 4, leftChild = [1,-1,3,-1], rightChild = [2,-1,-1,-1]
Output: true

Example 2:

Input: n = 4, leftChild = [1,-1,3,-1], rightChild = [2,3,-1,-1]
Output: false

Example 3:

Input: n = 2, leftChild = [1,0], rightChild = [-1,-1]
Output: false

Constraints:

• n == leftChild.length == rightChild.length

• 1 <= n <= 104

• -1 <= leftChild[i], rightChild[i] <= n - 1

codeSnippets.code:
class Solution(object):
    def validateBinaryTreeNodes(self, n, leftChild, rightChild):
        """
        :type n: int
        :type leftChild: List[int]
        :type rightChild: List[int]
        :rtype: bool
        """

hints: Find the parent of each node.
A valid tree must have nodes with only one parent and exactly one node with no parent.

solutions.explanation: Check: exactly one root (in_degree=0), all nodes have ≤1 parent, total edges=n-1.
